title: eriophyllum grandiflorum
intro: Eriophyllum grandiflorum, also known as the Oregon sunshine or woolly sun cups, is a perennial wildflower native to the western United States and southwestern Canada. This plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Eriophyllum grandiflorum are said to include the relief of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and cramps, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves for medicinal use, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
